assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Mark, Docklane has 15+ engineers in AM/GE/KZ and payment delays have already cost you time twice. Mellow takes the manual escalations off your plate: auto-payments, statuses without chat threads, a named owner when things break. Plus I can give you material for finance if you need to move it internally. What takes the most time in payment coordination right now?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: Ops impact with boundaries: manual payment follow-ups and reconciliation are replaced by Mellow-owned flow plus audit trail, while your team still owns legal qualification decisions. What would take the most pain off your team specifically?
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

objections.objection: Ok, you spotted the pain. But I already had one vendor who promised the same.
Specifically: what stops being manual? Give me hours and steps, not marketing language.

transcript.text: Mark, Docklane has 15+ engineers in AM/GE/KZ and payment delays have already cost you time twice. Mellow takes the manual escalations off your plate: auto-payments, statuses without chat threads, a named owner when things break. Plus I can give you material for finance if you need to move it internally. What takes the most time in payment coordination right now?
Ok, you spotted the pain. But I already had one vendor who promised the same.
Concretely for ops: less manual status chasing, less reconciliation overhead, and a named owner for incidents. That is the before/after change, not a generic platform claim.
Specifically: what stops being manual? Give me hours and steps, not marketing language.

Ops impact with boundaries: manual payment follow-ups and reconciliation are replaced by Mellow-owned flow plus audit trail, while your team still owns legal qualification decisions. What would take the most pain off your team specifically?
Ok. Send me something I can bring to finance without a long internal pitch.

Concretely for ops: less manual status chasing, less reconciliation overhead, and a named owner for incidents. That is the before/after change, not a generic platform claim. What else do you need for the next step?
The current setup is slow but I understand it. What specifically becomes more predictable?
Ops impact with boundaries: manual payment follow-ups and reconciliation are replaced by Mellow-owned flow plus audit trail, while your team still owns legal qualification decisions.
Send it. If it's concrete — I'll respond.
